prompt: Sealing a DatabaseDriver trait to prevent downstream impls.
broken_code: pub trait DatabaseDriver { fn connect(&self); } impl DatabaseDriver for () { fn connect(&self) {} } struct DownstreamDB; impl DatabaseDriver for DownstreamDB { fn connect(&self) {} }
error_message: Public traits can be implemented downstream.
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for () {} } pub trait DatabaseDriver: private::Sealed { fn connect(&self); } impl DatabaseDriver for () { fn connect(&self) {} }
explanation: By adding a `private::Sealed` supertrait, you prevent downstream crates from implementing `DatabaseDriver` because they cannot name or implement `private::Sealed`.

broken_code: pub trait HttpMethod { fn name(&self) -> &'static str; } pub struct Get; impl HttpMethod for Get { fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "GET" } } struct DownstreamMethod; impl HttpMethod for DownstreamMethod { fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "CUSTOM" } }
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Get {} } pub trait HttpMethod: private::Sealed { fn name(&self) -> &'static str; } pub struct Get; impl HttpMethod for Get { fn name(&self) -> &'static str { "GET" } }
explanation: The sealed trait pattern stops `DownstreamMethod` from being able to implement `HttpMethod`, securing the API boundaries.

broken_code: pub trait SerializationFormat {} pub struct Json; impl SerializationFormat for Json {} struct Xml; impl SerializationFormat for Xml {}
fixed_code: mod sealed { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Json {} } pub trait SerializationFormat: sealed::Sealed {} pub struct Json; impl SerializationFormat for Json {}
explanation: Creating a private `sealed` module with a `Sealed` trait makes the supertrait inaccessible to outside implementers.

broken_code: pub trait SocketType {} pub struct Tcp; impl SocketType for Tcp {} struct Udp; impl SocketType for Udp {}
fixed_code: mod sealed { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Tcp {} } pub trait SocketType: sealed::Sealed {} pub struct Tcp; impl SocketType for Tcp {}
explanation: The `SocketType` trait requires `sealed::Sealed`, effectively locking the trait implementations to the current crate.

broken_code: pub trait FileSystemBackend {} pub struct Local; impl FileSystemBackend for Local {} struct Remote; impl FileSystemBackend for Remote {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Local {} } pub trait FileSystemBackend: private::Sealed {} pub struct Local; impl FileSystemBackend for Local {}
explanation: Using a `private::Sealed` bounds enforces that only types authorized (i.e., defined within the same crate) can implement `FileSystemBackend`.

broken_code: pub trait CryptoAlgorithm {} pub struct Aes; impl CryptoAlgorithm for Aes {} struct Des; impl CryptoAlgorithm for Des {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Aes {} } pub trait CryptoAlgorithm: private::Sealed {} pub struct Aes; impl CryptoAlgorithm for Aes {}
explanation: A sealed trait prevents users of a library from adding their own potentially insecure or unsupported algorithms to an expected closed set.

broken_code: pub trait HashFunction {} pub struct Sha256; impl HashFunction for Sha256 {} struct Md5; impl HashFunction for Md5 {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Sha256 {} } pub trait HashFunction: private::Sealed {} pub struct Sha256; impl HashFunction for Sha256 {}
explanation: The sealed supertrait design is idiomatic in Rust for simulating 'closed' trait hierarchies.

broken_code: pub trait CompressionLevel {} pub struct Fast; impl CompressionLevel for Fast {} struct Custom; impl CompressionLevel for Custom {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Fast {} } pub trait CompressionLevel: private::Sealed {} pub struct Fast; impl CompressionLevel for Fast {}
explanation: Making `CompressionLevel` depend on `private::Sealed` guarantees the library only has to handle known compression levels.

broken_code: pub trait LogLevel {} pub struct Info; impl LogLevel for Info {} struct Verbose; impl LogLevel for Verbose {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Info {} } pub trait LogLevel: private::Sealed {} pub struct Info; impl LogLevel for Info {}
explanation: You protect the integrity of a logging framework's levels by sealing the `LogLevel` trait.

broken_code: pub trait MetricType {} pub struct Counter; impl MetricType for Counter {} struct Gauge; impl MetricType for Gauge {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Counter {} } pub trait MetricType: private::Sealed {} pub struct Counter; impl MetricType for Counter {}
explanation: By using a sealed trait, adding new metrics remains the purview of the crate author, averting breaking API changes.

broken_code: pub trait RenderBackend {} pub struct Vulkan; impl RenderBackend for Vulkan {} struct DirectX; impl RenderBackend for DirectX {}
error_message: Trait not sealed against foreign implementations
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Vulkan {} } pub trait RenderBackend: private::Sealed {} pub struct Vulkan; impl RenderBackend for Vulkan {}
explanation: A sealed `RenderBackend` trait stops consumers from passing completely unsupported backends to the renderer.

broken_code: pub trait PhysicsEngine {} pub struct Rapier; impl PhysicsEngine for Rapier {} struct CustomPhys; impl PhysicsEngine for CustomPhys {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Rapier {} } pub trait PhysicsEngine: private::Sealed {} pub struct Rapier; impl PhysicsEngine for Rapier {}
explanation: Sealing ensures physics integrations are rigorously controlled by the library maintainer.

broken_code: pub trait AudioCodec {} pub struct Mp3; impl AudioCodec for Mp3 {} struct Flac; impl AudioCodec for Flac {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Mp3 {} } pub trait AudioCodec: private::Sealed {} pub struct Mp3; impl AudioCodec for Mp3 {}
explanation: Sealed traits are perfect for APIs expecting a finite set of known variants at compile time.

broken_code: pub trait VideoCodec {} pub struct H264; impl VideoCodec for H264 {} struct Av1; impl VideoCodec for Av1 {}
error_message: Trait without bounds allows implementations in other crates
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::H264 {} } pub trait VideoCodec: private::Sealed {} pub struct H264; impl VideoCodec for H264 {}
explanation: By implementing the sealed pattern, adding a new variant requires modifying the crate itself.

broken_code: pub trait TransportProtocol {} pub struct Quic; impl TransportProtocol for Quic {} struct Http2; impl TransportProtocol for Http2 {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Quic {} } pub trait TransportProtocol: private::Sealed {} pub struct Quic; impl TransportProtocol for Quic {}
explanation: You can add methods to the sealed trait without breaking downstream code since nobody downstream can implement it.

broken_code: pub trait AuthenticationMethod {} pub struct Jwt; impl AuthenticationMethod for Jwt {} struct OAuth; impl AuthenticationMethod for OAuth {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Jwt {} } pub trait AuthenticationMethod: private::Sealed {} pub struct Jwt; impl AuthenticationMethod for Jwt {}
explanation: A sealed trait restricts implementors, establishing a robust boundary in complex domains like authentication.

broken_code: pub trait AuthorizationRole {} pub struct Admin; impl AuthorizationRole for Admin {} struct User; impl AuthorizationRole for User {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Admin {} } pub trait AuthorizationRole: private::Sealed {} pub struct Admin; impl AuthorizationRole for Admin {}
explanation: Roles shouldn't be extensible by third-party libraries for security reasons, making the sealed trait pattern essential.

broken_code: pub trait GuiWidget {} pub struct Button; impl GuiWidget for Button {} struct TextBox; impl GuiWidget for TextBox {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Button {} } pub trait GuiWidget: private::Sealed {} pub struct Button; impl GuiWidget for Button {}
explanation: If a GUI framework only supports a hardcoded set of widgets, sealing the trait reflects this constraint.

broken_code: pub trait LayoutManager {} pub struct Flex; impl LayoutManager for Flex {} struct Grid; impl LayoutManager for Grid {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Flex {} } pub trait LayoutManager: private::Sealed {} pub struct Flex; impl LayoutManager for Flex {}
explanation: For exhaustive pattern-matching equivalents on types, the sealed pattern acts similarly to non-exhaustive enums.

broken_code: pub trait EventType {} pub struct Click; impl EventType for Click {} struct Hover; impl EventType for Hover {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Click {} } pub trait EventType: private::Sealed {} pub struct Click; impl EventType for Click {}
explanation: Implementing `private::Sealed` ensures the `EventType` trait cannot be extended beyond the original author's intent.

broken_code: pub trait MessageQueue {} pub struct RabbitMq; impl MessageQueue for RabbitMq {} struct Kafka; impl MessageQueue for Kafka {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::RabbitMq {} } pub trait MessageQueue: private::Sealed {} pub struct RabbitMq; impl MessageQueue for RabbitMq {}
explanation: Using a sealed trait guarantees stable public APIs as the trait won't be implemented in ways that break future upgrades.

broken_code: pub trait CacheEvictionPolicy {} pub struct Lru; impl CacheEvictionPolicy for Lru {} struct Lfu; impl CacheEvictionPolicy for Lfu {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Lru {} } pub trait CacheEvictionPolicy: private::Sealed {} pub struct Lru; impl CacheEvictionPolicy for Lru {}
explanation: The supertrait `private::Sealed` locks down `CacheEvictionPolicy` to only types defined locally.

broken_code: pub trait LoadBalancingStrategy {} pub struct RoundRobin; impl LoadBalancingStrategy for RoundRobin {} struct Random; impl LoadBalancingStrategy for Random {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::RoundRobin {} } pub trait LoadBalancingStrategy: private::Sealed {} pub struct RoundRobin; impl LoadBalancingStrategy for RoundRobin {}
explanation: A sealed trait restricts dynamic extensions, standardizing behaviors across the entire system.

broken_code: pub trait TransactionIsolation {} pub struct Serializable; impl TransactionIsolation for Serializable {} struct ReadCommitted; impl TransactionIsolation for ReadCommitted {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Serializable {} } pub trait TransactionIsolation: private::Sealed {} pub struct Serializable; impl TransactionIsolation for Serializable {}
explanation: Sealed traits simulate a fixed set of implementations akin to a generic enum, useful for static dispatch.

broken_code: pub trait ThreadPoolType {} pub struct Fixed; impl ThreadPoolType for Fixed {} struct Cached; impl ThreadPoolType for Cached {}
fixed_code: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} impl Sealed for super::Fixed {} } pub trait ThreadPoolType: private::Sealed {} pub struct Fixed; impl ThreadPoolType for Fixed {}
explanation: Placing a `Sealed` bound that is defined inside a private module effectively closes the trait from arbitrary downstream implementations.
